steps.title: Set up the density formula
steps.content:
Population density equals total population divided by total area:
$$\text{Density} = \frac{\text{Population}}{\text{Area}} = \frac{2{,}278}{134} \text{ muskrats per acre}$$

steps.title: Backsolve to find the density
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (151):
If density is 151, then population $= 151 \times 134 = 20{,}234$ muskrats. This exceeds 2,278 far too much.

Backsolving — testing choice A (17):
If density is 17, then population $= 17 \times 134 = 17 \times 100 + 17 \times 34 = 1{,}700 + 578 = 2{,}278$ muskrats. This matches exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
